title: module_registry.tsv row — one biomod module's catalog state
description: Flat-TSV snapshot of `biomod status --json` for one module. Lets the atlas pages reason about MODULE readiness (installed / ready / stale) alongside DATA readiness (RESULT_READY / RUN_READY / MISSING / BLOCKED) without shelling out to biomod from the browser. Regenerated by scripts/sync_biomod_status.py from a `biomod status --json` payload (or a stub JSON file when biomod is not installed yet). One row per module per env. The biomod data model is the source of truth — see biomod v0 spec §'status JSON'.

module_name.description: biomod module name. FK target from analysis_modes.tsv.module_name.

installed.description: Is the module currently installed in the env.

ready.description: biomod ready = installed AND last_run.status='success' AND NOT stale.

stale.description: True if current inputs hash differs from last_run.inputs_hash.

stale_reason.description: Human-readable reason when stale=true. e.g. 'input vcf changed'.

parent.description: Parent module name for derivatives (per biomod parent/derivative resolution).

derivatives.description: Comma-separated list of derivative module names.

last_run_id.description: biomod run_id of the most recent run.

last_run_status.description: biomod runs.status of the last run.

last_run_qc.description: biomod runs.qc_status of the last run.

conda_env_path.description: biomod conda env path (~/.biomod/envs/<env>/conda/<module>/). Empty if the module declares no requirements.

biomod_env.description: Which biomod env this snapshot is from. Defaults to 'default'.

synced_at.description: When sync_biomod_status.py last refreshed this row.
